item.name: I002/000
item.title: Message Type
item.definition: This Data Item allows for a more convenient handling of the messages at the receiver side by further defining the type of transaction.
item.remark: Message Type values 1-127 are reserved for common standard use, whereas the values 128-255 are application dependent.

item.name: I002/010
item.title: Data Source Identifier
item.definition: Identification of the radar station from which the data are received.
item.remark: The defined SACs are listed in Part 1, Table 2. The defined SICs are listed in Part 1, Annex B.

item.name: I002/020
item.title: Sector Number
item.definition: Eight most significant bits of the antenna azimuth defining a particular azimuth sector.
item.remark: The use of the antenna azimuth as sector number has the advantage of being independent of the number of sectors implemented. Scale: 360/256 = 1.40625 degrees

item.name: I002/030
item.title: Time of Day
item.definition: Absolute time stamping expressed as UTC time.
item.remark: The time of day value is reset to zero each day at midnight. Scale: 1/128 seconds = 0.0078125 seconds

item.name: I002/041
item.title: Antenna Rotation Speed
item.definition: Antenna rotation period as measured between two consecutive North crossings or as averaged during a period of time.

item.name: I002/050
item.title: Station Configuration Status
item.definition: Information concerning the use and status of some vital hardware components of the radar system.
item.remark: Due to the diversity in hardware design and requirement of present and future radar stations, it is felt impractical to attempt to define the individual bits. This is a repetitive field with FX extension.

item.name: I002/060
item.title: Station Processing Mode
item.definition: Details concerning the present status with respect to processing parameters and options.
item.remark: Only the structure of this Data Item is defined, no attempt is made to standardise its contents. Typical information includes type of polarisation in use, MTI in use, presence of overload conditions. This is a repetitive field with FX extension.

item.name: I002/070
item.title: Plot Count Values
item.definition: Plot count values according to various plot categories, either for the last full antenna scan or for the last sector processed.
item.remark: First byte contains REP (repetition factor), followed by REP occurrences of 2-byte structures. A bit: 0=Counter for antenna 1, 1=Counter for antenna 2

item.name: I002/080
item.title: Warning/Error Conditions
item.definition: Warning/error conditions affecting the functioning of the radar system itself.
item.remark: Warning/error condition values 1-63 are reserved for common Standard use, whereas the values 64-127 are application dependent. This is a repetitive field with FX extension.

item.name: I002/090
item.title: Collimation Error
item.definition: Averaged difference in range and in azimuth for the primary target position with respect to the SSR target position as calculated by the radar station.
item.remark: RE (Range Error) scale: 1/128 NM. AE (Azimuth Error) scale: 360/16384 degrees = 0.02197265625 degrees. A default quantisation unit of 0.022° and a range between -2.8125° and +2.7905° is obtained.

item.name: I002/100
item.title: Dynamic Window - Type 1
item.definition: Signals the activation of a certain selective filtering function and in a polar coordinates system the respective geographical areas.
item.remark: RS (Rho Start), RE (Rho End): scale 1/128 NM, max value < 512 NM. TS (Theta Start), TE (Theta End): scale 360/65536 degrees. The logical meaning of the polar window is defined by its context, given by the Message Type (Data Item I002/000).

item.name: I002/SP
item.title: Special Purpose Field
item.remark: Special purpose field for application-specific data. First byte contains the length in bytes.
